title: delegate.py passes invalid --mcp-server flag to claude CLI
source_context: Running orchestrate.py sprint on WoningDossier project, 10 tickets across backend/frontend/architect/tester morty roles, all failed identically.
description: Sprint runs fail: every delegated Morty exits with code 1 and 'error: unknown option --mcp-se...'. The Claude CLI no longer accepts this flag (or its name changed). All 10 tickets in WoningDossier sprint were marked completed in phase 1 then immediately blocked in phase 2 with this error, meaning zero work was actually done but tracker reported success.
proposed_changes: Check current 'claude --help' for supported MCP flags. Likely needs to be removed or renamed (perhaps --mcp-config instead of --mcp-server). Also: sprint runner should NOT mark tickets done before verifying subprocess success — the phase-1 'completed' status is wrong when the underlying claude process failed.
